nameMeanings.name: Grevis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gunther, Helmut.
German: descriptive nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle High German grīs ‘gray’.

variants.name: Gries
meanings: South German: topographic name for someone who lived in a sandy place, from Middle High German griez ‘sand, pebble’.
Perhaps also a shortened form of German   Griese  .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Gries.

variants.name: Greiss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Hani, Kamal, Raouf, Rashad.
Coptic (Egypt): apparently from a respelling (of a derivative) of Egyptian Arabic garas ‘gong, bell’, denoting a bellringer. Compare Garas .

variants.name: Greiser
meanings: descriptive nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle High German grīs ‘gray’ (see Greis ).
habitational name for someone from Greis (Baden-Württemberg) and Greisau (Silesia).

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).
